entries.definition:
كتاب الهمزة

أخر - أخو

وأَخْوَتْ نُجومُ الأَخْذِ إلا أَنِضَّةً

أَنِضَّةَ مَحْلٍ ليس قاطرها يُثْرِي

(أخر) الهمزة والخاء والراءُ أصل واحدٌ إليه ترجع فروعُه، وهو خلاف التقدُّم. وهذا قياسٌ أخذْناه عن الخليل فإنّه قال: الآخِر نقيض المتقدّم. والأُخُر نقيض القُدُم، تقول مضى قُدُما وتأخَّرَ أُخُراً. وقال: وآخِرَة الرحل وقادمته ومُؤَخّر الرّحْل ومقَدّمه. قال: ولم يجئْ مُؤْخِر مخفّفة في شيء من كلامهم إلا في مُؤْخِر العين ومُقْدم العين فقط. ومن هذا القياس بِعتُك بيعاً بِأَخِرَةٍ أي نَظِرَة، وما عرفته إلا بأَخَرَة. قال الخليل: فعل الله بالأَخِرِ أي بالأَبْعد. وجئت في أُخْرَياتهم وأُخْرَى القوم. قال:

* أنا الذي وُلِدْتُ في أُخرَى الإبِلْ *

وابن دريد يقول: الآخِر تَالٍ للأوَّل. وهو قريبٌ ممّا مضى ذكره، إلاّ أنّ قولنا قال آخِر الرّجُلين وقال الآخِر، هو لقول ابن دريد أشد مُلاءمةً وأحسَنُ مطابقة. وأُخَرُ: جماعة أُخْرَى.

أَخْذَة ʾakhdhatun [n. of unit] a taking, a gripping; an instance of
taking to task; to seize; to take to task (69:10) فَعَصَوْا رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ
فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةً رَّابِيَةً but they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so
He seized them with an immense gripping.

آخِذ ʾākhidh [act. part.; pl. آخِذِين ʾākhidhīn] 1 one who takes or
accepts something (51:16) آخِذِينَ مَا آتَاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ they will be taking
what their Lord gives them 2 one who takes hold of, one who
controls (11:56) مَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ إِلاَّ هُوَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا there is no animal but
He is in complete control of it [lit. taking hold of its forelock].

اتِّخَاذ ʾittikhādh [v. n.] (act of) adopting or taking something to
be something else (2:54) إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ بِاتِّخَاذِكُمُ الْعِجْلَ [indeed] you
have wronged yourselves by taking the calf [for a deity].

مُتَّخِذ muttakhidh [act. part.; pl. مُتَّخِذِين muttakhidhīn; pl. fem.
مُتَّخِذَات muttakhidhāt] 1 one who takes for him/herself something
or someone (4:25) غَيْرَ مُسَافِحَاتٍ وَلاَ مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ not committing
adultery or taking lovers 2 one who takes someone for (18:51) وَمَا
كُنْتُ مُتَّخِذَ الْمُضِلِّينَ عَضُدًا and I would not take those who lead others
astray for supporters.
entries.definition_english:
أَخْذَة ʾakhdhatun [n. of unit] a taking, a gripping; an instance of
taking to task; to seize; to take to task (69:10) فَعَصَوْا رَسُولَ رَبِّهِمْ
فَأَخَذَهُمْ أَخْذَةً رَّابِيَةً but they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so
He seized them with an immense gripping.

آخِذ ʾākhidh [act. part.; pl. آخِذِين ʾākhidhīn] 1 one who takes or
accepts something (51:16) آخِذِينَ مَا آتَاهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ they will be taking
what their Lord gives them 2 one who takes hold of, one who
controls (11:56) مَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ إِلاَّ هُوَ آخِذٌ بِنَاصِيَتِهَا there is no animal but
He is in complete control of it [lit. taking hold of its forelock].

اتِّخَاذ ʾittikhādh [v. n.] (act of) adopting or taking something to

have wronged yourselves by taking the calf [for a deity].

مُتَّخِذ muttakhidh [act. part.; pl. مُتَّخِذِين muttakhidhīn; pl. fem.
مُتَّخِذَات muttakhidhāt] 1 one who takes for him/herself something
or someone (4:25) غَيْرَ مُسَافِحَاتٍ وَلاَ مُتَّخِذَاتِ أَخْدَانٍ not committing
adultery or taking lovers 2 one who takes someone for (18:51) وَمَا
كُنْتُ مُتَّخِذَ الْمُضِلِّينَ عَضُدًا and I would not take those who lead others
astray for supporters.

entries.definition:
ʾ–kh–r to delay, to postpone; to put behind; the end; the other,
the last. Of this root, 13 forms occur 250 times in the Qur’an: أَخَّرَ
ʾakhkhara 14 times; يُؤَخَّر yuʾakhkhar once; تَأَخَّرَ taʾakhkhara three
times; يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ yastaʾkhirūn six times; مُسْتَأْخِرِين mustaʾkhirīn once;
آخَر ʾākhar 15 times; آخَرَان ʾākharān twice; آخَرُون ʾākharūn 22
times; أُخْرَى ʾukhrā 26 times; أُخَر ʾukhar five times; آخِر ʾākhir 30
times; آخِرِين ʾākhirīn 10 times and الآخِرَة al-ʾākhira 115 times.

أَخَّرَ ʾakhkhara [v. II, trans.] 1 to delay, to defer (11:8) وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا
عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِلَى أُمَّةٍ مَّعْدُودَةٍ لَيَقُولُنَّ مَا يَحْبِسُهُ if We delay the chastisement
till a reckoned appointment they are sure to say, ‘What is holding
it up?’ 2 to grant respite, to respite, to reprieve (63:10) رَبِّ لَوْلاَ
أَخَّرْتَنِي إِلَى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ my Lord, if You would only reprieve me for a
little while 3 to put off, to leave undone (82:5) عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ
وَأَخَّرَتْ each soul will know what it has done and what it has left
undone.

يُؤَخَّر yuʾakhkhar [imperf. of pass. v. أُخِّرَ ʾukhkhira] to be

17
entries.definition_english:
ʾ–kh–r to delay, to postpone; to put behind; the end; the other,
the last. Of this root, 13 forms occur 250 times in the Qur’an: أَخَّرَ
ʾakhkhara 14 times; يُؤَخَّر yuʾakhkhar once; تَأَخَّرَ taʾakhkhara three
times; يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ yastaʾkhirūn six times; مُسْتَأْخِرِين mustaʾkhirīn once;
آخَر ʾākhar 15 times; آخَرَان ʾākharān twice; آخَرُون ʾākharūn 22
times; أُخْرَى ʾukhrā 26 times; أُخَر ʾukhar five times; آخِر ʾākhir 30
times; آخِرِين ʾākhirīn 10 times and الآخِرَة al-ʾākhira 115 times.

أَخَّرَ ʾakhkhara [v. II, trans.] 1 to delay, to defer (11:8) وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنَا
عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِلَى أُمَّةٍ مَّعْدُودَةٍ لَيَقُولُنَّ مَا يَحْبِسُهُ if We delay the chastisement
till a reckoned appointment they are sure to say, ‘What is holding
it up?’ 2 to grant respite, to respite, to reprieve (63:10) رَبِّ لَوْلاَ
أَخَّرْتَنِي إِلَى أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ my Lord, if You would only reprieve me for a
little while 3 to put off, to leave undone (82:5) عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ مَّا قَدَّمَتْ
وَأَخَّرَتْ each soul will know what it has done and what it has left
undone.

يُؤَخَّر yuʾakhkhar [imperf. of pass. v. أُخِّرَ ʾukhkhira] to be

17

entries.definition:
delayed, to be held back, to postpone (71:4) إِنَّ أَجَلَ اللَّهِ إِذَا جَاءَ لاَ يُؤَخَّرُ
when God’s appointed time arrives it cannot be postponed.

تَأَخَّرَ taʾakhkhara [v. V, intrans.] 1 to hold back, to lag behind
(74:37) لِمَن شَاءَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَتَقَدَّمَ أَوْ يَتَأَخَّرَ to those of you who choose to go
ahead or lag behind 2 to come after, to follow (48:2) لِيَغْفِرَ لَكَ اللَّهُ مَا
تَقَدَّمَ مِنْ ذَنْبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ that God may forgive what has gone past of
your sins and what comes after 3 to delay (2:203) وَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ فِي أَيَّامٍ
مَعْدُودَاتٍ فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ فَلاَ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَنْ تَأَخَّرَ فَلاَ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ
remember God on the appointed days, if anyone is in a hurry to leave after two
days, there is no blame on him, nor is there any blame on anyone
who delays.

يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ yastaʾkhirūn [imperf. of v. X, اِسْتَأْخَرَ ʾistaʾkhara,
intrans.] 1 to delay, to hold back (7:34) وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لاَ
يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً وَلاَ يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ there is an appointed time for every
people–when their appointed time comes they cannot hasten a
single moment nor delay 2 to remain behind (23:43) مَا تَسْبِقُ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ
أَجَلَهَا وَمَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ no community can go before its appointed time or
remain behind.

مُسْتَأْخِرِين mustaʾkhirīn [pl. of act. part. مُسْتَأْخِر mustaʾkhir] one
who is holding back, lagging behind (15:24) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَقْدِمِينَ مِنْكُمْ
وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَأْخِرِينَ and We know the ones of you who press forward
in the forefront and We know the ones who lag behind.

آخَر ʾākhar I [n./quasi-act. part.; dual آخَرَان ʾākharān, pl. آخَرُون
ʾākharūn; fem. أُخْرَى ʾukhrā (1) pl. أُخَر ʾukhar] 1 the other one
(12:36) قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَعْصِرُ خَمْرًا وَقَالَ الآخَرُ إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِي
خُبْزًا one of them said, ‘I saw myself pressing grapes’, and the
other said, ‘I saw myself carrying bread on my head’ 2 additional
or further one (39:68) ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَى then it is blown in it another
[time] II [adjectivally] 1 another/other (20:18) وَلِيَ فِيهَا مَآرِبُ أُخْرَى
and I have other uses for it 2 different (23:14) ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْنَاهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ
then We construct him into a different creation/creature 3
additional (6:19) أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً أُخْرَى do you really bear
witness that there are other gods beside God? 4 [derogative] ‘that
other one too!’ (53:19–20) أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللاَّتَ وَالْعُزَّى وَمَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الأُخْرَى
consider ʾallāt and ʾal-ʿuzzā, and that other, third, one–Manāt!

18

who is holding back, lagging behind (15:24) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَقْدِمِينَ مِنْكُمْ
وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَأْخِرِينَ and We know the ones of you who press forward
in the forefront and We know the ones who lag behind.

آخَر ʾākhar I [n./quasi-act. part.; dual آخَرَان ʾākharān, pl. آخَرُون
ʾākharūn; fem. أُخْرَى ʾukhrā (1) pl. أُخَر ʾukhar] 1 the other one
(12:36) قَالَ أَحَدُهُمَا إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَعْصِرُ خَمْرًا وَقَالَ الآخَرُ إِنِّي أَرَانِي أَحْمِلُ فَوْقَ رَأْسِي
خُبْزًا one of them said, ‘I saw myself pressing grapes’, and the
other said, ‘I saw myself carrying bread on my head’ 2 additional
or further one (39:68) ثُمَّ نُفِخَ فِيهِ أُخْرَى then it is blown in it another
[time] II [adjectivally] 1 another/other (20:18) وَلِيَ فِيهَا مَآرِبُ أُخْرَى

additional (6:19) أَئِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً أُخْرَى do you really bear
witness that there are other gods beside God? 4 [derogative] ‘that
other one too!’ (53:19–20) أَفَرَأَيْتُمُ اللاَّتَ وَالْعُزَّى وَمَنَاةَ الثَّالِثَةَ الأُخْرَى
consider ʾallāt and ʾal-ʿuzzā, and that other, third, one–Manāt!

18
